title: The Last Drop: From War to Coffee Shop Prices
subtitle: When global chaos meets your morning ritual

ripples.headline: Your Morning Coffee Now Costs $9.60/Pound, Up 31% Year-Over-Year

symphony_arc.opening: Distant thunder rolls across the Persian Gulf - a low, ominous bass note that sets the entire world stage trembling
symphony_arc.development: The rhythm builds as supply chains snap like piano wires under pressure, each break sending sharp staccato notes through global markets
symphony_arc.climax: A crescendo of chaos - oil tankers stranded, semiconductors stalled, fertilizer frozen - the full orchestra of modern commerce grinding to a discordant halt
symphony_arc.resolution: And in the quiet aftermath, somewhere in a coffee shop in Des Moines, a barista adjusts the chalkboard menu, adding fifty cents to a latte, while outside the world keeps turning

choices: Send the Navy to force the strait open
Drill domestically and let Europe deal with it
